Video.summary: In this Marathon Peak Performance, we take a look at Brandon Palaniuk's amazing 2022 season en route to his second Angler of the Year title.
Video.title: Peak Performance - Brandon Palaniuk

Video.summary: Cody Huff explores the benefits he's found when using various spoons during the late summer. He has dialed in his setups and doesn't experiment with multiple brands of spoons but rather uses the same spoons and changes sizes based on situation. From a magnum spoon to a compact jigging spoon Huff has found success on these from the Tennessee River to the Ozarks and many places in between.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Cody Huff

Video.summary: Brandon Cobb gives his top picks for effective baits on lakes with blueback herring. Spotted bass and largemouth will get hyper focused on blueback herring when they exist on a body of water and Cobb knows these baits will work throughout the year and not a certain specific season.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Brandon Cobb

Video.summary: Brandon Lester gives us two key factors he relies on when choosing between a Jig and Texas Rig which is often a debate in the Fall fishing season. Profile and rate of fall depending on the seasonal conditions are considerations for the two-time BASS winner.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Brandon Lester

Video.summary: John Crews offers a different option for enticing schooling fish to bite when it gets tough and pressured in the summer. Using a Spy Bait can offer up another look that is great for casting and mimicking baitfish.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - John Crews

Video.summary: Robert Gee reflects on his 2024 success with largemouth smallmouth and spotted bass noting that spots and smallmouth chase differently than largemouth requiring presentation adjustments. He shares his preferred head sizes go-to knot and a simple rod and reel setup most anglers can use.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Robert Gee

Video.summary: Lake Champlain Champion Ed Loughran runs through his jig setup that accounted for most of the fish throughout his tournament. He also leaned on a topwater in practice and on Championship Monday to catch smallmouth.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Ed Laughran III

Video.summary: Paul Marks shows off his single bait that produced in two different situations depending on how he rigged it. The Zoom Fluke Stick in Natural Shad contributed almost all of his weight in his Elite victory at Lake Hartwell. His first approach was rigging it weightless to throw on shallow points where bass pushed blueback herring to feed in the morning then he switch to a shakey head in the afternoons.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Paul Marks

Video.summary: Bill Lowen goes through two baits he always has rigged up this time of year. A tube and a jig are staples in Lowen's boat and he explains how both get fished in conjunction with one another. There are a few variables like pegging his weight or letting it free flow and also line size in these situations.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Bill Lowen

Video.summary: Matt Robertson talks about upsizing his spinnerbait and adjusting his blade sizes when throwing this lure in water temperatures varying from the 40's to 50's.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Matt Roberston

Video.summary: Shane LeHew talks about one of his favorite fun-fishing lures which is the Umbrella Rig aka Alabama Rig. LeHew utilizes this in multiple ways as he can cover water around shallow cover like he would with a spinnerbait but also with his electronics around deeper offshore structure.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Shane LeHew

Video.summary: Florida pro Bernie Schultz talks buzzbaits specifically two kinds. He keeps a squeaking buzzbait and clacking buzzbait ready to go at all times especially in the post-spawn. Although it's an excellent technique in the post-spawn Schultz acknowledges its use early in the year in places like Florida Texas and other southern states. Buzz it slow or fast make it clack or squeak add a toad or a trailer hook whatever you'd like with a buzzbait.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Bernie Schultz

Video.summary: Jacob Foutz walks us through his process of selecting sizes of jig heads when targeting bass in flooded timber. His sizes are as follows; 3/16 oz (5 to 10 feet) 1/4 oz (10 to 15 feet) 3/8 oz (20 feet and deeper). Foutz doesn't like getting any heavier than that if he can because of the leverage it gives fish to easily jump and throw the hook.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Jacob Foutz

Video.summary: Tyler Williams reveals the details to his 3/4 ounce jig he uses across the country. Upsizing his line can help slow the fall of his heavy jig along with pairing the jig with a big bulky trailer. Williams will use this jig around docks brush piles ledges and when targeting specific fish on his forward-facing sonar.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Tyler Williams

Video.summary: Alex Wetherell discusses two soft plastics he uses around his home in Connecticut and down south when the calendar hits December. Balls of bait equal bass for Wetherell as they actively feed even when the water temperatures are cooler at the end of the year. Bass aren’t nearly as lethargic as we once believed years ago.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Alex Wetherell

Video.summary: Stetson Blaylock relies on burning a crankbait over grass rock and stumps during late winter and pre-spawn. He ups his line size to keep the bait higher and uses a 7'4" rod for longer casts and ripping it free from grass.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Stetson Blaylock

Video.summary: Emil Wagner sheds light on his favorite bait for winter fishing especially on spotted bass lakes like Lanier. With jig head minnows neko rigs and dropshots becoming popular everyone forgot about the shaky head.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Emil Wagner

Video.summary: Greg Hackney gives some love to an often-forgotten lure for smallmouth fishing and that is the good ole fashioned tube. Hackney still appreciates what a tube brings to the table while fishing for smallmouth on competition days.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Greg Hackney

Video.summary: Easton Fothergill gives his daily adjustment to how fish were positioned each day of the Classic. From windy days to calm moments Fothergill kept adjusting his tackle to maximize efficiency.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Easton Fathergill

Video.summary: Pat Schlapper recounts his finest tournament week to date where to took the title at the Sabine River. Schlapper relied on a few areas of the Sabine along with a few lures including a Do-It Molds compact jig he pours himself altering it with a smaller stouter hook. He also utilized a Brazalo Custom Lures Bushwhacker Buzz Bait in the morning on the final day when it put multiple quality fish in the boat.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Pat Schlapper

Video.summary: Kyoya Fujita gives the specifics of his lures that helped him win at Toledo Bend. Fujita was using a technique called "mid-strolling" to get his Jackall Drift Fry and his Deps Sakamata Shad to the correct depth to target suspended bass.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Kyoya Fujita

Video.summary: Justin Hamner gives his strategy to catch key bass each day of competition and how he utilized multiple kinds of jerkbaits and mixed in a jig to catch the majority of his weight on Grand Lake in the 2024 Bassmaster Classic.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Justin Hamner

Video.summary: Bill Lowen runs down his three favorite colors for squarebill crankbaits in the fall. Another essential aspect to a squarebill that Lowen cherishes is a circuit board bill vs a plastic bill. It gives the crankbait the feel and characteristic of a balsa crankbait while maintaining the durability of a plastic crankbait.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Bill Lowen

Video.summary: John Garrett runs down the three baits that were key for his win on the Harris Chain of Lakes. Garrett rotated between these techniques depending on how the fish were positioned and their aggressiveness on the shell bar.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - John Garrett

Video.summary: Joey Cifuentes tackles where the fish go when they finish spawning but the water temperatures aren't at their hottest yet like they will be later in the summer. Cifuentes dives into three different baits that are useful around lakes with standing timber for the last week of May as we head into June.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Joey Cifuentes III

Video.summary: Brock Mosley praises the most versatile lure in bass fishing the Yamamoto Senko. He shows how you can quickly switch from fishing the Senko weightless wacky rigged to adding a weight and changing the hook positioning for a Neko Rig. This lure will get fish to bite every day of the year depending on how you rig it.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Brock Mosley

Video.summary: Brad Whatley gives his strategy of covering water around the spawn when there is thicker cover present. Whatley winds his weighted swimbait around spawning areas and will trigger strikes or boils revealing where bass are positioned to catch on another tactic if necessary.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Brad Whatley

Video.summary: Jason Christie discusses the tricky period immediately after bass leave the bed and are either guarding fry or recovering from the spawn. Here is how Christie opts to catch them.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Jason Christie

Video.summary: Joey Cifuentes talks about the shallow nature of fish even when the temperatures are dropping across the country. The first feeling anglers have when the water temperatures drop is to fish deeper water but Cifuentes mentions how even in the Ozarks bass will hang out around shallow wood and rock still keyed in on a bigger meal that imitates dying baitfish.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Joey Cifuentes III

Video.summary: Brandon Card gives his thoughts on early season jerkbait fishing. Card gives scenarios when he will opt for a different cadence for his jerkbait rod length and line size.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Brandon Card

Video.summary: Lake Fork Champion Trey McKinney gives his lure arsenal as he adjusted daily to the changing fish for the second stop of the Elite Series season. Early in the event he used a jerkbait and jig head minnow combo but as the weather warm and conditions changed his baits adapted. Culminating on the final day were multiple baits contributed to his 130-15 tournament weight.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Trey McKinney

Video.summary: Kyle Patrick gives his approach for fishing for less pressured fish during the spawning months of the year. The fishing myth around the spawn is that bass have to go to the bank to do their yearly spawn when in reality fish can spawn much deeper and will take longer to do so.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Kyle Patrick

Video.summary: Elite Series Champion Patrick Walters gives the details of his approach at Lake Murray. Walters relied on a few baits throughout the week to win the event wire to wire. When fish were active and schooling Walters utilized a Zoom Super Fluke and a Rapala Jowler.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Patrick Walters

Video.summary: Shane LeHew relies on a jerkbait from late fall through the spawn whether using electronics or not. He keeps two color options: translucent for sunny or clear water and solid for cloudy or stained conditions.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Shane LeHew

Video.summary: Jake Whitaker talks about the shallow bite windows that open up even with 100 degree days in the south. Whitaker leans on a topwater toad around any sort of cover that could hold fish. Anglers may get less bites in the heat of the summer but they can be the quality you need in a tournament.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Jake Whitaker

Video.summary: Bryan New goes through some of the keys he likes in his jig when targeting bass in the prespawn. New breaks down his jig trailer and why it's efficient and also explains an adjustment to his jig size that he used to never use.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Bryan New

Video.summary: Caleb Sumrall talks about one of the best ways to catch big bass. Sumrall strongly believes in a frog but when bass start shying away or missing it he downsizes instead of changing baits completely. When conditions change bass moods can vary and with that topwater becomes a high risk high reward situation and Sumrall will downsize to the keep the odds in his favor.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Caleb Sumrall

Video.summary: Bernie Schultz gives his three trusted lures when he is covering water to develop a pattern in the spring. He balances a spinnerbait swim jig and chatterbait when he approaches different types of cover.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Bernie Schultz

Video.summary: Logan Parks discusses a strategy he employed at Wheeler during his first Top 10. Varying line size can help crankbaits run different depth and retrieved faster or slower. It's something he recommends during the summer months when schools are forming offshore or have been fished for frequently.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Logan Parks

Video.summary: Jason Christie discusses fishing a jerkbait around Christmas time and how two of his baits act totally different. Christie breaks down how the Smithwick Rogue and Booyah Flash Point react different on the same cadence. Water temperatures in the 40's Christie opts for the Rogue. Meanwhile he sees positive results with the quick-working Flash Point with temperatures in the 50's and even 60's.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Jason Christie

Video.summary: Taku Ito goes through two key baits to his Bassmaster Elite Series victory at Smith Lake. From Neko Rigging a bluegill imitating bait to dropshotting a Magnum Fuzzy Dice he managed the biggest bass and bag to win.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Taku Ito

Video.summary: Logan Latuso discusses a big key in the summer months...SHADE. This underrated aspect of the summer can get you some of the biggest bites of your day and it seemingly gets better as the day progresses. Take notes from Latuso and don't run your shade pattern too early to ensure some of your best success.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Logan Latuso

Video.summary: Austin Felix gives his thoughts on when he picks up a Dropshot versus a Ned Rig and a Neko Rig. Speed is often the determining factor for each presentation and he tends to reserve the Neko for largemouth situations.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Austin Felix

Video.summary: Bob Downey goes through what he would consider his spring selection for largemouth fishing. Spring means March and April in the south but can mean April May and June up north. Downey opts for a crankbait swim jig and two types of soft plastics when making the spring transition.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Bob Downey

Video.summary: Bob Downey dishes out a few of his must-have baits for the early fall fishing season for Smallmouth bass. Downey who lives in Minnesota knows how incredible the bite can be this time of year before cold temps hit daily. Given the type of lake Downey has options for you in this video.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Bob Downey

Video.summary: Cliff Prince dials us in on his 1/2 ounce Chatterbait setup that helped him win the Bassmaster Elite at Wheeler Lake. He also dishes the details on his magic spot and why he thinks the morning was incredible every day for him.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Cliff Prince

Video.summary: Matt Arey talks about the importance of varying your retrieve with swimbaits over structure like brush piles. This is something that can be done with or without forward facing sonar as Arey describes the trial and error used prior to newer technology.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Matt Arey

Video.summary: Bill Lowen explains his two-pronged approach at the St. Johns River sticking to one area for four days using a black and blue swim jig and a flipping jig. He highlights his swim jig cadence and the importance of his flipping jig falling into hard cover
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Bill Lowen

Video.summary: Seth Feider goes through his wood jig and grass jig setup. He explains the differences in line tie and weed guard why he likes a short hook and his colors for flipping. Whether it's docks laydowns shoreline grass or submerged vegetation; Feider will often rely on a jig to get it done. His favorite size is a 5/8 ounce.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Seth Feider

Video.summary: Brandon Palaniuk shares the three baits he used to win the Bassmaster Elite Series at Lake Okeechobee: a Megabass Kanata jerkbait and two Megabass crankbaits (Big M and a prototype). Fishing one spot aggressively led to some of the biggest strikes seen on Bassmaster LIVE.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Brandon Palaniuk

Video.summary: Cory Johnston goes over his two main lures for bed fishing at the St. Johns River en route to his first Elite Series victory. Johnston also explains his use of Perspective Mode when seeing bed fish in low visibility times.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Cory Johnston

Video.summary: Cory Johnston gives a quick bait breakdown of a key lure that helped him win the 2024 Bassmaster Elite Series event at the St. Lawrence River. Johnston battled the waves out in Lake Ontario on the final two days of the event to secure a win his second of the season.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Cory Johnston

Video.summary: Kyle Welcher gives his take on a few productive lures in the prespawn and how he balances their uses to be most effective while on the water. Should you use a Chatterbait where a jig could also work or vice versa he attacks this and more in this scenario.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Kyle Welcher

Video.summary: Todd Auten goes over three Chatterbait variations that have made the South Carolina pro successful with this technique. Whether it's color selection trailer options or getting the bait to ride high in the water column or fall fast Auten has his process. 1. White/CChartuese w/Zoom Shimmer Shad 2. Black/Blue w/Zoom ZCraw or Green Pumpkin Fluke 3. Firecraw w/Speed Craw
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Todd Auten

Video.summary: Stetson Blaylock explains a new bait he was introduced to that helps him catch not only deep northern smallmouth but also southern largemouth in the winter months. He also discusses the value in 2D sonar with this technique around structure in the south.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Stetson Blaylock

Video.summary: Hunter Shryock talks about one of the most fun techniques and lures that is so effective in the Fall season. Covering water with a Berkley Choppo is essential and allows Shryock to burn through dead water in no time with this approach and it is a tournament winning formula.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Hunter Shryock

Video.summary: Bryan New lays out one of his favorite topwater presentations the Buzz Toad. Pioneered by his good friend and fellow Elite Todd Auten New likes to use this bait to imitate multiple types of forage bass will eat in the post-spawn.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Bryan New

Video.summary: Tucker Smith talks about finding hard spots on Lake Fork with his side imaging throughout practice and then using his forward facing sonar to execute and catch fish throughout the tournament. He utilized a 7-inch minnow on spinning tackle with varying head sizes as well as a 3/4-ounce football jig on the final day.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Tucker Smith

Video.summary: Scott Martin gives us his version of Thanksgiving turkey and fixings with this three-bait lineup this time of year. He has a bait for every level of the water column depending on how the fish are acting and where you're fishing this time of year.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Scott Martin

Video.summary: Scott Martin sets up the calendar for Florida's Spawn Cycle. Depending on weather spawn can start in south Florida as early as Thanksgiving week and extend through May of the next year. Martin gives his take on the difference of a lake like Okeechobee and the St. Johns River farther north.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Scott Martin

Video.summary: Hunter Shryock discusses the rising trend of using a swimbait in the prespawn as a substitute for commonly thrown baits with similar profiles. Fished around the same shallow cover it can trigger the big kicker bite tournament anglers seek.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Hunter Shryock

Video.summary: Justin Atkins sheds light on the flats. A vast area of consistent contour depths and also a place where baitfish and bass congregate as the season turns to cooler temperatures in the fall. This can be a great landing spot for bass as they get positioned to feed during this time of year.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Justin Atkins

Video.summary: Clifford Pirch gives us a few ways to rig a drop shot if you're specifically targeting smallmouth and spotted bass. Threading his worm on weedless is his ideal setup but when fishing for smallmouth and spotted bass they tend to be more open water and less obstructions around. That's when Pirch will opt for a few other methods for fall rate and action.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Clifford Pirch

Video.summary: John Cox gives a look at two of his favorite topwater lures and ones he trusts in the Fall. Whether it's around grass wood or rock both of these lures are on his front deck this time of year. Cox experimented with a longer frog rod this season and it helped him land a 3rd place finish at Wheeler Lake.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - John Cox

Video.summary: Bryan Schmitt highlights how different SPRO Aruku Shad versions and hook sizes enhance performance based on season cover and action. He adjusts for fall rates with yo-yo or straight retrieves and downsizes when the bite is tough.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Bryan Schmitt

Video.summary: Kyle Welcher breaks down his single bait that produced a record winning margin and over 118 pounds of bass at the Pasquotank River. Flipping a Rapala CrushCity Bronco Bug to shallow roamers and spawning bass was his strategy. He utilized his forward facing sonar to pick out fish among the cover littered on the bottom.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Kyle Welcher

Video.summary: Bryan Schmitt pulls 4 swim jigs out of his tackle box to showcase their effectiveness on different pieces of cover and depths. A simple trailer change can help Schmitt's swim jig stay shallow or go deeper. Schmitt gives the nuts and bolts to swim jig fishing his way and style.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Bryan Schmitt

Video.summary: One of the best structure fishermen in the world Brandon Palaniuk talks about isolated structure in the fall season. BP also gives a few bait suggestions including his handy dropshot.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Brandon Palaniuk

Video.summary: The Shad Spawn is a unique stage of the fishing season that bridges the gap from the bass spawn into the post spawn. Shad spawning around hard shallow cover gives bass a great opportunity to feed heavily in a short window of time. Not all places in the country feature a shad spawn but if threadfin are present in your lake a shad spawn is likely to happen. Low-light conditions early in the morning are helpful and baits like a spinnerbait imitate small shad perfectly to load the boat for Greg Hackney in a hurry.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Greg Hackney

Video.summary: Tim Dube walks us through one of his favorite techniques in tournaments and fun fishing. Dube gives his take on practicing with a Glide Bait and his strategy to convert followers into fish that will commit to biting.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Timothy Dube

Video.summary: Jason Williamson provides a solution to those tricky bass that are days from spawning and sit high in the water column. Not only does a jig provide great opportunities for good catches but a swimbait also entices those big females to commit.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Jason Williamson

Video.summary: Kenta Kimura gives some suggestions to be better with the Megabass Magdraft swimbait. He changes the treble to his signature Ryugi quad-hook design for better hookups and he also adds a split ring to the line tie for increased head action and roll. Those two things are essential for Kimura along with finding the right speed on retrieve.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Kenta Kimura

Video.summary: Matt Herren goes through the process of fishing current seams on rivers with a spinnerbait. No matter time of year water current is a great place to find fish and target where bass are positioned and if you find the correct lineup it can be fast action. Herren recommends a 1/2-ounce to 3/4-ounce compact spinnerbait to avoid snags and hangups and control the bait despite the current pushing and washing the spinnerbait.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Matt Herren

Video.summary: Jason Williamson pulls out his favorite bait for the fall when the water temperatures are falling and in many situations the water level is falling too. When the bass start keying in on crawfish with cooling water J-Will knows its jig season. With living rubber skirted jigs Williamson fishes a 1/2 ounce or 3/4 ounce jig and never goes lighter than 1/2 ounce. He will also target brush early and as it continues to cool down will move to rock through the winter.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Jason Williamson

Video.summary: Wes Logan talks moving water and how he approaches it. Being from the Coosa River Logan is used to fishing moving water no matter the time of year and these three baits help him have good success.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Wes Logan

Video.summary: Tim Dube discusses his use of a deep diving jerkbait to cover water depths from 4 feet out to 15 feet or so. Dube exclusively uses 10 to 12-pound line and will change his cadence from fast early in the retrieve to slower in the strike zone.
Video.title: Tackle Tip - Timothy Dube

Video.summary: Recognized as the GOAT of the sport, Kevin VanDam came on the scene and dominated in every way with 4 Bassmaster Classic Titles and 7 Angler of the Year wins. KVD was always in contention for championships and holds the record for most professional wins at 25.
Video.title: B.A.S.S. Superstar: Kevin VanDam

Video.summary: Texas has consistently hosted B.A.S.S. events with some of the most memorable and iconic fish catches in the history of tournament bass fishing.
Video.title: B.A.S.S. Giant Bass in Texas

Video.summary: People recognize Davy today as one of the most important voices in the sport of Bass Fishing. Davy’s experience on the water though, produced 8 professional level wins including 2 Angler of the Year titles and 1 Bassmaster Classic.
Video.title: B.A.S.S. Superstar: Davy Hite

Video.summary: The B.A.S.S. Federation has been the heartbeat of the sport. It has been and always will be the way for weekend warriors to compete and possibly win on the biggest stage.
Video.title: B.A.S.S. From Grass Roots to the Biggest Stage

Image.alt: B.A.S.S. From Grass Roots to the Biggest Stage
B.A.S.S. From Grass Roots to the Biggest Stage
B.A.S.S. From Grass Roots to the Biggest Stage

Video.summary: Some of the biggest changes in the sport happened starting in the late 90s and into the new millennium. The way the sport was covered and viewed on television exploded to new levels never seen before.
Video.title: B.A.S.S. Tournaments Meet Turn of the Century

Image.alt: B.A.S.S. Tournaments Meet Turn of the Century
B.A.S.S. Tournaments Meet Turn of the Century
B.A.S.S. Tournaments Meet Turn of the Century

Video.summary: Revered by many as “The Natural” in professional bass fishing, Aaron Martens brought a unique approach to the sport reaching the highest level. 3 Angler of the Year Titles and 9 Professional level wins proved his techniques were quite effective in the sport.
Video.title: B.A.S.S. Superstar: Aaron Martens

Video.summary: Considered one of the most prestigious awards in the sport of professional Bass fishing, this episode highlights what it takes to be a consistent champion in the highest level of professional bass fishing.
Video.title: The Cast
